trade.summary: France and Poland have significant trade ties within the EU single market. France exports machinery, automobiles, and agri-food to Poland. Poland exports machinery, automotive parts, and furniture to France. Poland is an important production location for French manufacturers.

diplomatic.summary: France and Poland are NATO and EU allies but have diverged on several strategic issues. Poland prioritizes hard US security guarantees over French-led European autonomy. The Weimar Triangle (France, Germany, Poland) is an important format but underutilized. Post-PiS Poland under Tusk has warmed relations with Brussels and Paris. Ukraine policy now closely aligned.
recent_incidents: disagreements on EU strategic autonomy vs. transatlantic reliance
Polish skepticism of French Africa policy

military.summary: Both NATO members. France has deployed troops to Poland as part of NATO enhanced forward presence. Poland's large defence buildup creates opportunities for French arms sales. Poland purchased South Korean and American systems over French alternatives, but dialogue on Eurofighter/Rafale continues. EUROCORPS cooperation active.

financial.summary: France is one of Poland's top foreign investors, with major presence in banking (BNP Paribas, Credit Agricole), retail (Auchan, Leroy Merlin), and manufacturing. Polish investment in France is limited. Strong supply chain integration through EU single market.

energy.summary: France has offered nuclear cooperation to Poland, which is planning its first nuclear power plants. EDF and French nuclear expertise are competing for contracts. Cooperation in renewables growing. Poland's coal dependency contrasts with French nuclear-led electricity model.

scientific.summary: Strong academic ties through EU research programmes. Polish students are a significant presence in French universities. Joint research in mathematics (Polish mathematical tradition), physics, and social sciences. Copernicus Centre for Interdisciplinary Studies has French collaboration.
